question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the /network/status endpoint with concurrent unauthenticated requests, causing RetrieveLatest() and RetrieveGenesis() to queue up and exhaust database connection pools, thereby stalling block ingestion and delaying the latest block timestamp by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential calls to RetrieveGenesis() and RetrieveLatest() within a single NetworkStatus() invocation to amplify database load, causing both queries to block simultaneously and delaying the reported CurrentBlockTimestamp beyond 500% of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: GetTimestampMillis()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger integer truncation in GetTimestampMillis() (ConsensusStartNanos / int64(time.Millisecond)) by crafting requests that cause the block repository to return a ConsensusStartNanos value near an int64 boundary, producing a wildly incorrect CurrentBlockTimestamp that makes downstream Rosetta clients believe a block has been delayed by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of /network/status requests with no rate limiting enforced in networkAPIService, causing the addressBookEntryRepo.Entries() call to saturate I/O resources and indirectly delay block finalization by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of request-level timeout enforcement in NetworkStatus() to hold open long-lived connections that exhaust goroutine or thread pools, causing block processing pipelines to stall and the next block to be delayed by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call /network/options to force repeated iteration over types.GetTransactionResults() and persistence.IsTransactionResultSuccessful(), causing CPU saturation that delays block processing and pushes the next block timestamp beyond 500% of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkList()] Can an unprivileged external user send a sustained flood of /network/list requests, which despite being lightweight individually, collectively saturate the HTTP server's accept queue and prevent block-related requests from being processed, causing a block delay exceeding 500% of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NetworkStatus() calls RetrieveLatest() which returns the most recently finalized block, and by flooding the system before a new block is finalized, cause the CurrentBlockTimestamp to remain stale for a duration exceeding 500% of the average block time as observed by Rosetta clients?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a /network/status request with an oversized or malformed MetadataRequest body that causes the context passed to RetrieveGenesis() and RetrieveLatest() to be cancelled mid-flight, leaving partial database transactions open and delaying subsequent block writes by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NewNetworkAPIService()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated re-initialization paths in NewNetworkAPIService() by exploiting service restart conditions caused by resource exhaustion attacks, causing the operationTypes slice to be rebuilt repeatedly and delaying block transaction processing by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of caching on the RetrieveLatest() call in NetworkStatus() to cause repeated expensive database queries on every request, creating a thundering herd effect that delays block finalization and pushes the next block beyond 500% of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the peers response from addressBookEntryRepo.Entries() being included in NetworkStatus() to infer the full peer topology, then target individual peers with DoS attacks that reduce consensus participation and delay the next block by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user use the CurrentBlockTimestamp returned by NetworkStatus() to precisely time a burst attack at the moment a new block is expected, flooding the system exactly when block finalization is occurring and causing the block to be delayed by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the errors.Errors slice exposed in NetworkOptions() to enumerate all internal error codes and craft targeted requests that reliably trigger expensive error-handling paths, causing block processing delays exceeding 500% of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a context that carries a very long deadline, causing NetworkStatus() to hold database connections open for extended periods via RetrieveGenesis() and RetrieveLatest(), starving block ingestion processes of connections and delaying the next block by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential (non-parallel) execution of RetrieveGenesis() followed by RetrieveLatest() followed by addressBookEntryRepo.Entries() in NetworkStatus() to triple the per-request database latency, and through concurrent flooding cause cumulative delays that push block finalization beyond 500% of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: GetTimestampMillis()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a scenario where ConsensusStartNanos is zero or negative (e.g., by triggering an edge case in block repository), making GetTimestampMillis() return 0 or a negative timestamp, causing Rosetta clients to treat the network as frozen with a block delay of effectively infinite percentage above the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that IsOnline() only checks if blockRepo is non-nil (not if it is healthy or responsive) to craft conditions where blockRepo exists but is unresponsive, causing NetworkStatus() to hang indefinitely and block processing to stall beyond 500% of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly invoke /network/options to force repeated allocation of the operationStatuses slice (make([]*rTypes.OperationStatus, 0, len(types.GetTransactionResults()))) on every call, causing GC pressure that introduces stop-the-world pauses long enough to delay block processing by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any block-time anomaly detection in NetworkStatus() — which simply returns CurrentBlockTimestamp without comparing it to the 24-hour average — to silently observe a block delay exceeding 500% of the average block time without any alert being raised, allowing an ongoing freeze to persist undetected?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the peers.ToRosetta() conversion in NetworkStatus() to trigger a large memory allocation if the address book contains many entries, causing OOM pressure that delays block processing goroutines and pushes the next block beyond 500% of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user use the GenesisBlockIdentifier returned by NetworkStatus() to craft block-range queries that span the entire chain history, causing full-table scans in the block repository that compete with block ingestion I/O and delay the next block by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NetworkStatus() returns the CurrentBlockIdentifier (index and hash) to enumerate all block hashes and mount a targeted hash-collision or cache-poisoning attack against the block repository, causing block lookups to fail and delaying the next block by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of response size limits in NetworkStatus() — particularly the Peers field from addressBookEntryRepo.Entries() — to cause the server to serialize and transmit an unbounded peer list, exhausting network bandwidth and delaying block propagation by 500% or more of the average block time?
